codeReview.summary: The scanner parses valid SKILL.md files and populates counts, but two persistence bugs make the database diverge from the filesystem after normal scans/rescans.

issues.description: scan_all_skills_impl persists installed_path as SKILL.md (skill.file_path) instead of the platform entry path/directory. The schema and install flow expect installed_path to be the path under the platform skills directory, so every scanned installation record is wrong for copied/native skills and any detail UI built on top of this metadata will display the wrong installation path.

scan_all_skills_impl only upserts discovered rows and never removes stale skills or skill_installations that disappeared from disk. After a user deletes or renames a skill and rescans, get_skills_by_agent/get_central_skills can still return the old database rows, so the persisted state no longer matches the filesystem.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The tauri-rust-worker handoff already notes the skill procedure says to use the deprecated tempdir crate; the implementation correctly used tempfile instead.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoffs/2026-04-09T09-12-21-406Z__rust-scanner-and-parser__1751db2c-466d-4943-a44f-24695c917336.json:150-152 suggested updating the skill docs because tempfile/TempDir is the current crate.

summary: Fail. Scanner persistence writes the wrong installed_path and never cleans up stale rows on rescan.
